# Front Matter {.unnumbered}

::: {.content-visible unless-format="pdf" unless-format="epub"}

## Access and Formats

This book is available in multiple formats for a range of reading preferences and uses.

### Digital

- **HTML:** Version of Record. 
- **EPUB:** Download via the <i class="bi bi-download"></i> icon in the left-hand sidebar.
- **PDF:** Download via the <i class="bi bi-download"></i> icon in the left-hand sidebar.

### Print 

The paperback edition is available through major online retailers and bookstores. 

- [Amazon](https://www.amazon.com/Making-Indiana-University-History-Landscape/dp/B0GVW1DZXT/ref=sr_1_1?sr=8-1)
- [Barnes & Noble](https://www.barnesandnoble.com/w/making-indiana-university-james-capshew/1149822098?ean=9798218794088)
- [Lulu](https://www.lulu.com/shop/james-capshew/making-indiana-university/paperback/product-m2z5krk.html?q=making+indiana+university&page=1&pageSize=4)

**ISBN (paperback):** 979-8-218-79408-8

### Source Files 

Source files are available via the **View source** link in the right-hand sidebar.

- **Quarto Markdown (QMD)**
- **TeX** (`Making-Indiana-University.tex`)

:::

::: {.content-visible unless-format="pdf"}

## Copyright, License, and Permissions

&copy; 2025 James H. Capshew

<p xmlns:cc="http://creativecommons.org/ns#" xmlns:dct="http://purl.org/dc/terms/"><a property="dct:title" rel="cc:attributionURL" href="https://iulibrariespublishing.iu.edu/making-IU/">Making Indiana University: History, Landscape, and a Sense of Place</a> by <a rel="cc:attributionURL dct:creator" property="cc:attributionName" href="https://hpsc.indiana.edu/about/emeriti-faculty/capshew-james.html">James H. Capshew</a> is licensed under <a href="https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/?ref=chooser-v1" target="_blank" rel="license noopener noreferrer" style="display:inline-block;">CC BY-NC-ND 4.0</a><img style="height:22px!important;margin-left:3px;vertical-align:text-bottom;" src="https://mirrors.creativecommons.org/presskit/icons/cc.svg?ref=chooser-v1" alt="Creative Commons Icon" /><img style="height:22px!important;margin-left:3px;vertical-align:text-bottom;" src="https://mirrors.creativecommons.org/presskit/icons/by.svg?ref=chooser-v1" alt="Creative Commons Attribution 'By' Icon" /><img style="height:22px!important;margin-left:3px;vertical-align:text-bottom;" src="https://mirrors.creativecommons.org/presskit/icons/nc.svg?ref=chooser-v1" alt="Creative Commons NonCommercial Icon" /><img style="height:22px!important;margin-left:3px;vertical-align:text-bottom;" src="https://mirrors.creativecommons.org/presskit/icons/nd.svg?ref=chooser-v1" alt="Creative Commons NoDerivatives Icon" /> unless otherwise indicated.</p>

The illustrations were created by Joe Lee as works made for hire and are owned by their copyright holder, James H. Capshew.

## Publisher

![](images/iubl_pub_logo.png){fig-alt="Logo of Indiana University Bloomington Libraries Publishing" width="40%"}\

[Indiana University Bloomington Libraries Publishing](https://libraries.indiana.edu/libraries-publishing)\
1320 E. Tenth Street\
Bloomington, IN 47405

## Cover Information

**Cover Image**: Joe Lee, 2024; &copy; 2024 James H. Capshew\
**Cover Design**: Adam Mazel

:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}

## Abstract

This book sheds light on the creation of Indiana University's institutional identity and image over its two centuries of existence by investigating the role of historians, archivists, and others in documenting its historical record. As such, it is an exercise in historiography, or the study of the history of IU history. The first part presents a rationale for an inclusive view of contributors to IU history, including not only historians and archivists but also architects, groundskeepers, and other members of its academic community. Essays on the contributions of the first professor as well as the invention of the genre of Indiana University history provides a useful provenance. The second section supplies a chronological study of the history of IU's distinctive campus design, from its beginning in 1885 to 2020, to illustrate the essential role that place plays in university culture. The third and final part are essays that uncover hidden efforts to sustain the university's historical record in publications, faculty memorials, and historic preservation. By its interrogation of the sources and methods that construct the historical record, this book makes a unique contribution to the study of Indiana University history and culture.  

:::
